frameworks.name: traveling waves of cortical oscillations
frameworks.one_line: oscillations form spatially organized patterns whose peaks move across cortex

frameworks.name: stationary neural oscillations
frameworks.one_line: alternative view that cortical oscillations are spatially fixed rather than propagating

core_question: Do prefrontal cortical oscillations form traveling waves and are they modulated by working memory demands?
key_assumptions: multi-electrode PFC recordings can resolve spatial phase structure
theta, alpha, beta bands index distinct cortical processes
manipulations: baseline vs working memory task performance
key_findings: PFC oscillations in theta, alpha, beta bands organized as traveling waves
baseline waves flowed bidirectionally along a specific axis
task performance increased directional bias, especially in beta
interpretations: traveling waves are a structured spatiotemporal substrate of PFC activity modulated by cognitive demand
different frequency bands carry independently directed wave flows
